note: sost-cli now accepts global flags in any position. Previously, anything after the subcommand (e.g. 'sost-cli send sost1... 0.01 --capsule-mode structured ...') was silently dropped because the flag parser stopped at the first non-flag token. The TX cf1a90138d... was confirmed today as a victim: it went out without a capsule despite the --capsule-mode / --capsule-text / --capsule-template flags being present, simply because they came after 'send'. The rewritten parser scans the entire argv once, consumes known global flags wherever they appear, and gathers everything else (subcommand + positional args + any flag the subcommand handles itself) into a compacted argv. Two-arg flags now also fail loudly when the value is missing, instead of silently consuming the next flag as the value. The TX detail panel was already correct in v164 — re-run the APP rewards send and the capsule will appear this time. Force-reload (Ctrl+Shift+R).
